title: Counselling (Seniors)
text:
Counselling (Seniors) - SupportGoWhere
Counselling (Seniors)
Share link
Provides counselling to older persons aged 50 and above and/or their caregivers, to improve their mental well-being and resilience to cope with life’s challenges.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based, Online
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Gerontological counselling provides an avenue for seniors and their caregivers to discuss their issues and concerns and help to address their personal, social or psychological challenges.

Individual and group counselling available.

Counselling can be provided at the counselling centre or at their own home if necessary.

Who is this for?

Persons aged 50 years old and above with issues and concerns such as:

Grief and loss

Loneliness/isolation

Depression

Stress and anxiety

Relationship problems

Difficulty adjusting to transitions and changes (e.g. retirement, ‘empty nest’ syndrome, onset of illnesses)




Family members/caregivers who experience stress caring for their elderly loved ones who are experiencing the above-mentioned concerns can also be supported. 




Hua Mei Centre for Successful Ageing also provides individual and group coaching in emotion management, inter-generational relationship-building and communication skills. 

What to expect?

The counselling centre will arrange an appointment for you with a professional counsellor who will assess your needs and advise if counselling is able to meet your needs.

If counselling is not suitable, the counsellor will refer you to other appropriate services.

A counselling session takes around 1 to 1.5 hours.

Most issues will need more than 1 counselling session to resolve. 

Fees payable for Hua Mei Centre for Successful Ageing and O’Joy Limited. 

How to apply?

For enquiries or appointment booking, call or email the respective service provider.

Information last updated on 09 Dec 2025
